courseleaf.title: Anthropology, Master of Arts
courseleaf.informationtext:
Graduate Study

The Department offers a program of study leading to M.A. and Ph.D. degrees in Anthropology. Further information regarding graduate study may be obtained at the Department office and at Graduate Studies.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Anthropology.

Advising

To connect with a graduate advisor, see Graduate Advising.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Students gain Knowledge of the evolution of primate and human behavior, behavioral ecology of nonhuman primates, cultural & human evolution, hunter-gatherers, North American prehistory, archaeological theory, identity politics, critical theory,  gender and society,  and urban anthropology. Students graduate with skills necessary for professional research and teaching in archaeology, linguistic anthropology, biological anthropology, or social anthropology.
